p.strong: When component combinations multiply beyond control

g.text: Sparse Set: Rare component, dynamic add/remove
Hybrid: Mix both based on access patterns

div.p: Archetype explosion is a design problem, not an algorithm problem. The solution isn't better archetype management—it's better component design. Group related data, use bitflags for markers, and reserve sparse sets for rare components. Healthy ECS: 10-50 archetypes, 100+ entities per archetype Fragmented ECS: 500+ archetypes, <10 entities per archetype
Possible combinations: 2¹⁰ = 1,024 archetypes With 10,000 entities: ~10 entities per archetype Result: Cache thrashing

ol.li: Visit archetype A01 (2 entities) → Cache miss: load 64 bytes → Process 2, waste 90% of cache line

Visit archetype A03 (3 entities)
→ Cache miss: load 64 bytes → Process 3, waste 85%
... repeat for 50+ archetypes Result: 50 cache misses for 100 entities vs. 2 cache misses with dense archetype

ul.li: Iterator setup: ~10 cycles With 50 matching archetypes: 50 × 30 cycles = 1,500 cycles overhead before processing any data vs. Dense archetype: 1 × 30 cycles = 30 cycles overhead

div.p: Components added/removed without consideration of resulting archetype count Example: Adding DebugTag to random entities creates new archetypes for each combination
Each boolean flag as separate component doubles potential archetypes IsPlayer, IsEnemy, IsNPC, CanBeDamaged, IsVisible... → 2⁵ = 32 variants
Adding Dialogue component to 5 NPCs out of 1000 entities creates 5 singleton archetypes if each has different base component set
Result: Single EntityFlags component replaces 4+ marker components
Target ratio: entities / archetypes > 50 If below 20, investigate fragmentation

□ Do these components always appear together? → Combine into struct □ Is this a boolean flag? → Use bitflags in existing component □ Is this component rare (<5% of entities)? → Use sparse set instead □ Does adding this create <10 new archetypes? → Acceptable, monitor □ Does this vary per entity or per archetype? → Per-archetype: consider as resource
🚨 Archetype count grows during gameplay 🚨 Many archetypes with 1-3 entities 🚨 Query iteration visits 20+ archetypes 🚨 Adding a component takes >1ms 🚨 Memory usage grows unexpectedly
Health MaxHealth Armor Resistance → 2⁴ = 16 archetypes for combat stats alone
CombatStats { health: f32, max_health: f32, armor: f32, resistance: f32 } → 1 archetype
